features.name: Globally Load Balanced CDN
features.notesEn:
- 320 cities in 120+ countries, including mainland China
- 13,000 networks directly connect to Cloudflare, including every major ISP, cloud provider, and enterprise
- 280 Tbps global network edge capacity, consisting of transit connections, peering and private network interconnects
- ~50 ms from about 95% of the world’s Internet-connected population
Link: https://www.cloudflare.com/en-gb/network/ and https://developers.cloudflare.com/reference-architecture/architectures/cdn/#cloudflare-cdn-archi

features.name: Static Content Caching
features.notesEn:
Cloudflare only caches based on file extension and not by MIME type. The Cloudflare CDN does not cache HTML by default. Additionally, Cloudflare caches a website’s robots.txt.
https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/concepts/default-cache-behavior/
If you want to customize: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/troubleshooting/customize-caching/
Review cache performance and analytics: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/performance-review/

features.name: Cache Bypass On Cookie
features.notesEn: Migrated to Cache Rules: https://developers.cloudflare.com/rules/reference/page-rules-migration/#migrate-bypass-cache-on-cookie/

features.name: Custom Cache Keys
features.notesEn: A Cache Key is an identifier that Cloudflare uses for a file in our cache, and the Cache Key Template defines the identifier for a given HTTP request: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/how-to/cache-keys/#create-custom-cache-keys

features.name: Min Cache TTL Expiry (Edge)
features.notesEn: Edge Cache TTL (Time to Live) specifies the maximum time to cache a resource in the Cloudflare global network. Edge Cache TTL is not visible in response headers and the minimum Edge Cache TTL depends on plan type.

features.name: Min Cache TTL Expiry (Browser)
features.notesEn:
The Browser Cache TTL sets the expiration for resources cached in a visitor’s browser. By default, Cloudflare honors the cache expiration set in your Expires and Cache-Control headers but overrides those headers if:

- The value of the Expires or Cache-Control header from the origin web server is less than the Browser Cache TTL Cloudflare setting.
- The origin web server does not send a Cache-Control or an Expires header.

features.name: Client Max Upload Size (MB)
features.notesEn: The upload limit for the Cloudflare API depends on your plan. If you exceed this limit, your API call will receive a 413 Request Entity Too Large error.

features.name: TCP Turbo
features.notesEn:
“TCP Turbo”(enabled automatically) automatically chooses the TCP settings to further accelerate your website.
Accelerate latency with custom-tuned TCP optimizations

features.name: Instant Full Cache Purge
features.notesEn:
Cloudflare offers different options to purge your content. Although single-file cache purging (purge by URL) is recommended, the following options are also available:
https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/how-to/purge-cache/
​Purge by single-file / Purge everything / Purge cache by cache-tags / Purge cache by hostname / Purge cache by prefix (URL) / Purge cache key resources / P​urge varied images

features.name: Prioritized IP Ranges
features.notesEn: Enterprise Internet assets are placed on Cloudflare dedicated IP ranges, providing prioritized routing and protection to ensure maximum speed and availability. Enterprise customers get better traffic routing, it’s less likely for Enterprise customers to face routing issues compared to Business, Pro and Free.

features.name: Prefetching URLs
features.notesEn: URL prefetching means that Cloudflare pre-populates the cache with content a visitor is likely to request next. This setting — when combined with additional setup — leads to a higher cache hit rate and thus a faster experience for the user: https://developers.cloudflare.com/speed/optimization/content/prefetch-urls/

features.name: Argo Smart Routing
features.notesEn:
Argo Smart Routing detects real-time network issues and routes traffic across the most efficient network path. These benefits are most apparent for users farthest from your origin server: https://developers.cloudflare.com/argo-smart-routing/#cloudflare-argo-smart-routing 

Argo for Packets dynamically chooses the best possible path through Cloudflare’s network and looks at every path back from every Cloudflare data center back to your origin, down to the individual network path. Argo compares ex

features.name: Tiered Caching
features.notesEn: Tiered Cache works by dividing Cloudflare’s data centers into a hierarchy of lower-tiers and upper-tiers. If content is not cached in lower-tier data centers (generally the ones closest to a visitor), the lower-tier must ask an upper-tier to see if it has the content. If the upper-tier does not have the content, only the upper-tier can ask the origin for content. This practice improves bandwidth efficiency by limiting the number of data centers that can ask the origin for content, which reduces

features.name: Load Balancing
features.notesEn: Cloudflare Load Balancing distributes traffic across your servers, which reduces server strain and latency and improves the experience for end users: https://developers.cloudflare.com/load-balancing/understand-basics/

features.name: Global Load Balancing (Geo-based Routing)
features.notesEn: Geo steering directs traffic to pools tied to specific countries, regions, or — for Enterprise customers only — data centers. This option is extremely useful when you want site visitors to access the origin server closest to them, which improves page-loading performance.

features.name: Load Balancing Health Checks
features.notesEn: Check for healthy or unhealthy origins: https://developers.cloudflare.com/load-balancing/understand-basics/health-details/

features.name: Stream
features.notesEn: Cloudflare Stream lets you or your end users upload, store, encode, and deliver live and on-demand video with one API, without configuring or maintaining infrastructure. You can use Stream to build your own video features in websites and native apps, from simple playback to an entire video platform: https://developers.cloudflare.com/stream/#cloudflare-stream

features.name: Stream Delivery
features.notesEn: You run any regular HLS or DASH hosting server and CF intelligently caches video parts. This allows you to keep using regular video infrastructure without depending on Cloudflare too much, while still using the powerful gloabal datacenter network of Cloudflare: https://www.cloudflare.com/en-gb/products/stream-delivery/

features.name: Accelerated Mobile Links (AMP)
features.notesEn: Accelerated Mobile Links automatically identifies links on a Cloudflare customer's site to content with an AMP version (Google technology) available. If a link is clicked from a mobile device, the AMP content will be loaded nearly instantly.

features.name: IPv6, HTTP/2, SPDY
features.notesEn:
SPDY is the core part of HTTP/2 protocol, many of the http/2 protocol techniques are part SPDY. SPDY(speedy) is a network protocol which manipulates http protocol by compressing headers, predicting clients requests(example discussed below) and other techniques, to fasten web experience.

Cloudflare leverages these protocols to speed up the website and application on Internet

features.name: WebSockets
features.notesEn: WebSockets are used for real-time, event-driven communication between clients and servers. They are particularly useful for building software applications requiring instant updates, such as real-time chat, messaging, and multiplayer games.

features.name: Async JavaScript Loading with Rocket Loader
features.notesEn:
Rocket Loader prioritizes your website’s content (text, images, fonts, and more) by deferring the loading of all of your JavaScript until after rendering. This type of loading (known as asynchronous loading) leads to earlier rendering of your page content. Rocket Loader handles both inline and external scripts, while maintaining order of execution. Cloudflare will detect incompatible browsers and disable Rocket Loader.
On pages with JavaScript, this results in a much faster loading experience fo

features.name: Image Optimization with Polish
features.notesEn: Cloudflare Polish is a one-click image optimization product that automatically optimizes images in your site. Polish strips metadata from images and reduces image size through lossy or lossless compression to accelerate the speed of image downloads. When an image is fetched from your origin, our systems automatically optimize it in Cloudflare’s cache. Subsequent requests for the same image will get the smaller, faster, optimized version of the image, improving the speed of your website: https://

features.name: Image Resizing
features.notesEn: Belongs to Image Transformation:  Transformations let you optimize and manipulate images stored outside of the Cloudflare Images product. Transformed images are served from one of your zones on Cloudflare: https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/transform-images/

features.name: Mobile Optimization (Cloudflare Mirage)
features.notesEn:
Cloudflare Mirage reduces bandwidth used by images in mobile browsers. It can accelerate loading of image-heavy websites on very slow mobile connections and HTTP/1.
https://developers.cloudflare.com/speed/optimization/images/mirage/#cloudflare-mirage 
It does this by:
- Replacing images with low-resolution thumbnails, which are bundled together into one file.
- Acting as a lazy loader, deferring loading of higher-resolution images until they become visible.
* JavaScript must be enabled for Mirag

features.name: CNAME Setup
features.notesEn: CNAME flattening speeds up CNAME resolution and allows you to use a CNAME record at your root/apex domain (example.com): https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/cname-flattening/set-up-cname-flattening/

features.name: Subdomain Zones (LTZ)
features.notesEn: Allow to create different zone with subdomain

features.name: Header Rewrites
features.notesEn:
Used to rewrite headers if customers don’t have a way of controlling their headers. For example, if your Amazon S3 bucket requires a specific host header set in order to fetch assets. Need to migrate to Cloudflare Rules
https://developers.cloudflare.com/rules/reference/page-rules-migration/#migrate-host-header-override

features.name: Key-value Storage with Workers
features.notesEn: Workers KV is a data storage that allows you to store and retrieve data globally. With Workers KV, you can build dynamic and performant APIs and websites that support high read volumes with low latency: https://developers.cloudflare.com/kv/

features.name: Unmetered DDoS Mitigation
features.notesEn: Cloudflare’s unmetered mitigation of DDoS stops illegitimate volumetric traffic at the Cloudflare edge. All Cloudflare plans include unmetered mitigation without fear of being dropped. (Cloudflare protects its own edge locations and infrastructure)

features.name: Enterprise DDoS Mitigation
features.notesEn: Cloudflare’s enterprise-grade mitigation of DDoS attacks against layers 3, 4, & 7 includes prioritized IP ranges and routing, ensuring maximum speed and availability. Available for ENT plan

features.name: True Client IP Header
features.notesEn: True-Client-IP provides the original client IP address to the origin web server. True-Client-IP is only available on an Enterprise plan.

features.name: Cloudflare Access - Basic
features.notesEn:
Zero Trust access for all of your applications, replace traditional VPN: https://developers.cloudflare.com/learning-paths/replace-vpn/concepts/ 
- Authenticate users on our global network
- Onboard third-party users seamlessly
- Log every event and request

features.name: Mutual TLS
features.notesEn: Mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication uses client certificates to ensure traffic between client and server is bidirectionally secure and trusted. mTLS also allows requests that do not authenticate via an identity provider — such as Internet-of-things (IoT) devices — to demonstrate they can reach a given resource: https://developers.cloudflare.com/learning-paths/application-security/default-traffic-security/mtls/#mutual-tls-mtls

features.name: Spectrum (for TCP/UDP)
features.notesEn: Spectrum allows you to route MQTT, email, file transfer, version control, games, and more over TCP or UDP through Cloudflare to mask the origin and protect it from DDoS attacks: https://developers.cloudflare.com/spectrum/#cloudflare-spectrum

features.name: Static IPs, Anycast Your Own IP
features.notesEn: Protect your own IP with Cloudflare, but need assessment from Cloudflare before the IP is propagated

features.name: Deploy Intelligence to Identify New Threats
features.notesEn: Cloudflare handles millions of HTTP requests each second and blocks billions of cyber threats each day. Cloudflare uses that data to detect malicious actors on the Internet and turns that information into a list of known malicious IP addresses. Cloudflare also integrates with a number of third-party vendors to augment the coverage.

features.name: Reputation-based Threat Protection
features.notesEn: The threat score is a score from 0 (zero risk) to 100 (high risk) classifying the IP reputation of a visitor. IP reputation is calculated based on Project Honeypot, external public IP information, as well as internal threat intelligence from WAF managed rules and DDoS: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/custom-rules/use-cases/block-ip-reputation/#block-requests-by-threat-score

features.name: Comment Spam Protection
features.notesEn: Block spam to your website

features.name: Content Scraping Protection
features.notesEn: Protect website from scrapping content

features.name: API Protection - API Shield
features.notesEn: Cloudflare offers a range of products to help identify and address API vulnerabilities. Cloudflare offers the following features to help secure, management and monitoring your APIs: API Discovery / Volumetric Abuse Detection / Sequence Analytics / Sequence Mitigation / GraphQL malicious query protection / JSON Web Tokens Validation / Mutual TLS (mTLS) / Schema Validation: https://developers.cloudflare.com/api-shield/

features.name: Page Shield
features.notesEn: Ensures the safety of your website visitors’ browser environment. Page Shield simplifies external script management by tracking loaded resources like scripts and providing alerts when it detects new resources or malicious scripts. Page Shield also tracks the connections made by scripts on your domain’s pages and checks if they are malicious based on their destination: https://developers.cloudflare.com/page-shield/#cloudflare-page-shield

features.name: Domain Protection
features.notesEn: Custom Domain Protection for Cloudflare Registrar, available on the Enterprise Plan, protects your organization from domain hijacking with exclusively out-of-band verification of any changes to your Registrar account. Cloudflare is an ICANN accredited registrar providing secure domain registration for high-profile domains.

features.name: DDoS Alerts
features.notesEn: Cloudflare will send you a real-time alert when our systems automatically detect and mitigate a DDoS attack: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ddos-protection/reference/alerts/#ddos-alerts

features.name: Spectrum DDoS Protection for SSH / Minecraft
Advanced Bot Management
features.notesEn: Stop bad bots from Credential Stuffing, Content Scraping/Spamming, Inventory Hoarding and Credit Card Stuffing types of attacks by leveraging smart data. Bot Management for Enterprise is a paid add-on that provides sophisticated bot protection for your domain. Customers can identify automated traffic, take appropriate action, and view detailed analytics within the dashboard: https://developers.cloudflare.com/bots/get-started/bm-subscription/

features.name: Captcha/JS Challenge
features.notesEn:
When a website is protected by Cloudflare, there are several occasions when it will challenge visitor traffic:
- The visitor’s IP address has shown suspicious behavior online (as tracked by Project Honeypot).
- The website owner has blocked the country associated with the visitor’s IP address.
- The visitor’s actions have activated a WAF custom rule enabled by the website owner.
If the visitor passes the challenge, their request is allowed. If they fail, the request will be blocked: https://deve

features.name: Bot Fight Mode
features.notesEn:
Bot Fight Mode is a simple, free product that helps detect and mitigate bot traffic on your domain. When enabled, the product:
- Identifies traffic matching patterns of known bots
- Issues computationally expensive challenges in response to these bots
- Notifies Bandwidth Alliance partners (if applicable) to disable bots
https://developers.cloudflare.com/bots/get-started/free/

features.name: Super Bot Fight Mode
features.notesEn:
Super Bot Fight Mode (Pro) is included in your Pro subscription. When enabled, the product:
- Identifies traffic matching patterns of known bots
- Can challenge or block bots
- Offers protection for static resources
- Provides limited analytics to help you understand bot traffic
https://developers.cloudflare.com/bots/get-started/pro/ 

Super Bot Fight Mode (Business) is included in your Business or Enterprise subscription. When enabled, the product:
- Identifies traffic matching patterns of know

features.name: DNS Firewall
features.notesEn: Cloudflare DNS Firewall proxies all DNS queries to your nameservers through Cloudflare’s global network. This action protects upstream nameservers from DDoS attacks and reduces load by caching DNS responses: https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/dns-firewall/#dns-firewall

features.name: Universal SSL Certificate
features.notesEn:
By default, Cloudflare issues — and renews — free, unshared, publicly trusted SSL certificates to all domains added to and activated on Cloudflare.
The process for activating a Universal SSL certificate depends on your domain’s DNS setup: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ssl/edge-certificates/universal-ssl/enable-universal-ssl/#enable-universal-ssl-certificates

features.name: Keyless SSL
features.notesEn: Keyless SSL allows security-conscious clients to upload their own custom certificates and benefit from Cloudflare, but without exposing their TLS private keys. Keyless SSL is only available to Enterprise customers that maintain their own SSL certificate purchased from a valid Certificate Authority. Cloudflare does not supply any certificates for use with Keyless SSL: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ssl/keyless-ssl/#keyless-ssl

features.name: Bring Your Own SSL Certificate (Custom SSL)
features.notesEn:
Custom certificates are meant for Business and Enterprise customers who want to use their own SSL certificates.
Unlike Universal SSL or advanced certificates, Cloudflare does not manage issuance and renewal for custom certificates: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ssl/edge-certificates/custom-certificates/

features.name: Cloudflare For SaaS
features.notesEn:
Cloudflare for SaaS allows you to extend the security and performance benefits of Cloudflare’s network to your customers via their own custom or vanity domains: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-for-platforms/cloudflare-for-saas/ 

As a SaaS provider, you may want to support subdomains under your own zone in addition to letting your customers use their own domain names with your services. For example, a customer may want to use their vanity domain app.customer.com to point to an appli

features.name: Advanced Certificate Manager
features.notesEn: Advanced Certificate Manager is a flexible and customizable way to issue and manage certificates in Cloudflare. Use Advanced Certificate Manager when you want something more customizable than Universal SSL but still want the convenience of SSL certificate issuance and renewal. For example, use Advanced Certificate Manager to cover more than one level of subdomain, remove Cloudflare branding from the Universal certificate, or adjust the shortest certificate lifespan: https://developers.cloudflare

features.name: Does WAF need to be contracted separately to receive access to WAF features and benefits?
features.notesEn: WAF is a separated features so must be contracted separately or the features you receive are only belong to Free plan

features.name: Number of Custom WAF Rules
features.notesEn: Custom rules allow you to control incoming traffic by filtering requests to a zone. You can perform actions like Block or Managed Challenge on incoming requests according to rules you define : https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/custom-rules/

features.name: Security Analytics
features.notesEn:
Security Analytics displays information about all incoming HTTP requests for your domain, including requests not handled by Cloudflare security products: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/analytics/security-analytics/ 

Use the Security Analytics dashboard to:
- View the traffic distribution for your domain.
- Understand which traffic is being mitigated by Cloudflare security products, and where non-mitigated traffic is being served from (Cloudflare global network or origin server).
- Analyz

features.name: Free Managed Ruleset
features.notesEn: Available on all Cloudflare plans. Designed to provide mitigation against high and wide impacting vulnerabilities. The rules are safe to deploy on most applications. If you deployed the Cloudflare Managed Ruleset for your site, you do not need to deploy this managed ruleset: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/

features.name: Cloudflare Managed Ruleset
features.notesEn: Created by the Cloudflare security team, this ruleset provides fast and effective protection for all of your applications. The ruleset is updated frequently to cover new vulnerabilities and reduce false positives: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/reference/cloudflare-managed-ruleset/

features.name: OWASP Core Ruleset
features.notesEn: The Cloudflare OWASP Core Ruleset is Cloudflare’s implementation of the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS). Cloudflare routinely monitors for updates from OWASP based on the latest version available from the official code repository: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/reference/owasp-core-ruleset/

features.name: WAF Attack Score - Machine Learning Detection
features.notesEn: WAF attack score allows you to identify these attack variations and their malicious payloads. It classifies each request using a machine learning algorithm, assigning an attack score from 1 to 99 based on the likelihood that the request is malicious: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/about/waf-attack-score/#waf-attack-score

features.name: Exposed Credentials Check
features.notesEn:
The WAF provides two mechanisms for this check: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/check-for-exposed-credentials/#check-for-exposed-credentials 
- The Exposed Credentials Check Managed Ruleset, which contains predefined rules for popular CMS applications. By enabling this ruleset for a given zone, you immediately enable checks for exposed credentials for these well-known applications.
- The ability to write custom rules at the account level that check for exposed credentials acc

features.name: Sensitive Data Detection
features.notesEn:
The Cloudflare Sensitive Data Detection managed ruleset helps identify data leaks generated by your origin servers. Its rules run on the body of the response looking for patterns of common sensitive data, including:
- Personal identifiable information (PII) — for example, passport numbers
- Financial information — for example, credit card numbers
- Secrets — for example, API keys
https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/reference/sensitive-data-detection/

features.name: WAF Content Scanning
features.notesEn: When enabled, content scanning attempts to detect content objects, such as uploaded files, and scans them for malicious signatures like malware. The scan results, along with additional metadata, are exposed as fields available in WAF custom rules, allowing you to implement fine-grained mitigation rules: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/about/content-scanning/

features.name: Number of Custom Lists (account level)
features.notesEn: Create custom lists of IP addresses, hostnames, and ASNs to use and reference in your custom WAF rules: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/tools/lists/custom-lists/#custom-lists

features.name: Rate Limiting (IP-based)
features.notesEn: With IP-based rate limiting, you can limit the number of requests a certain IP address can make, and prevent abuse from "repeat offender" IP addresses. Enterprise customers can negotiate a reduced rate through custom pricing.

features.name: Rate Limiting Rules
features.notesEn: Rate limiting rules allow you to define rate limits for requests matching an expression, and the action to perform when those rate limits are reached.: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/rate-limiting-rules/#rate-limiting-rules

features.name: Advanced Rate Limiting
features.notesEn:
With advanced rate limiting, you can write rate limiting rules using all the fields of the HTTP request to protect against denial-of-service attacks, brute-force login attempts, API traffic surges and other types of abuse targeting APIs and applications. With Advanced Rate Limiting, you can:
- Define the rule filter using all HTTP request characteristics, such as URI, method, headers, cookies and body fields. Customers on a Bot Management plan get access to the bot score dynamic field too. You c

features.name: Rate Limiting Counting Periods
features.enterprise: 10 s, 1 min, 2 min, 5 min, 10 min, 1h (ENT with Advanced Rate Limiting)
features.notesEn: A maximum number of requests that a user or client can make within a given time period: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/rate-limiting-rules/#rate-limiting-rules

features.name: Rate Limiting Timeout Periods
features.enterprise: 10 s, 1 min, 2 min, 5 min, 10 min, 1 h, 1 day
features.notesEn: A maximum number of interval that a user or client can make: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/rate-limiting-rules/#rate-limiting-rules

features.name: Account Level WAF
features.notesEn: Manage WAF configurations for all of your domains

features.name: User Agent Blocking Rules
features.notesEn: User Agent Blocking rules block specific browser or web application  User-Agent request headers. These rules apply to the entire domain instead of individual subdomains. User Agent Blocking rules are applied after Zone Lockdown rules. If you allow an IP address via Zone Lockdown, it will skip any User Agent Blocking rules: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/tools/user-agent-blocking/#create-a-user-agent-blocking-rule

features.name: Zone Lockdown Rules
features.notesEn: Zone Lockdown specifies a list of one or more IP addresses, CIDR ranges, or networks that are the only IPs allowed to access a domain, subdomain, or URL. You can configure multiple destinations, including IPv4/IPv6 addresses, in a single Zone Lockdown rule. All IP addresses not specified in the Zone Lockdown rule will not have access to the specified resources. Requests from those IP addresses will receive an Access Denied response: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/tools/zone-lockdown/#crea

features.name: Payload inspection
features.notesEn: The WAF allows you to log the request information that triggered a specific rule of a managed ruleset. This information is known as the payload. Payload logging is especially useful when diagnosing the behavior of WAF rules. Since the values that triggered a rule may contain sensitive data, they are encrypted with a customer-provided public key so that only you can examine them later: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/managed-rules/payload-logging/

features.name: Log/Simulate traffic before execute (testing purpose)
features.notesEn: To test for false positives, set WAF managed rules to Simulate mode. This mode allows you to record the response to possible attacks without challenging or blocking incoming requests. Also, use the Firewall Analytics Activity log to determine which managed rules caused false positives: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waf/reference/legacy/old-waf-managed-rules/troubleshooting/#troubleshoot-false-positives

features.name: Global Anycast DNS
features.notesEn:
Powered by CF infrastrustrure:
- 320 cities in 120+ countries, including mainland China
- 13,000 networks directly connect to Cloudflare, including every major ISP, cloud provider, and enterprise
- 280 Tbps global network edge capacity, consisting of transit connections, peering and private network interconnects
- ~50 ms from about 95% of the world’s Internet-connected population
Link: https://www.cloudflare.com/en-gb/network and https://developers.cloudflare.com/reference-architecture/architect

features.name: DNSSEC
features.notesEn:
DNS Security Extensions (DNSSEC) adds an extra layer of authentication to DNS, ensuring requests are not routed to a spoofed domain.
https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/dnssec/#dnssec

features.name: Custom Nameservers
features.notesEn: With custom (or vanity) nameservers, a domain can use Cloudflare DNS without using Cloudflare-branded nameservers. For instance, you can configure ns1.example.com and ns2.example.com as nameservers for example.com: https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/nameservers/custom-nameservers/#custom-nameservers

features.name: Wildcard DNS Record
features.notesEn: Wildcard DNS records allow you to have a many-to-many mapping, for example if you had hundreds or thousands of subdomains you wanted to point to the same resources. Wildcard records are used as the response for all subdomains that are not specifically covered by another DNS record: https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/manage-dns-records/reference/wildcard-dns-records/#wildcard-dns-records

features.name: Wildcard DNS Record Proxy
features.notesEn: Cloudflare supports proxied wildcard DNS records at all plan levels. A proxied wildcard DNS record allows you to proxy non-existing subdomains providing security and performance for all subdomains covered by the proxied wildcard DNS record.

features.name: Secondary DNS
features.notesEn: With incoming zone transfers, you can keep your primary DNS provider and use Cloudflare as a secondary DNS provider: https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/zone-setups/zone-transfers/cloudflare-as-secondary/#secondary-dns---incoming-zone-transfers

features.name: Waiting Room
features.notesEn: Cloudflare Waiting Room allows you to route excess users of your website to a customized waiting room, helping preserve customer experience and protect origin servers from being overwhelmed with requests: https://developers.cloudflare.com/waiting-room/#cloudflare-waiting-room

features.name: Multi-User Administrative Access
features.notesEn: Can invite or administrator and set permissions/roles for them

features.name: Role-based Access Control
features.notesEn: Role-based access for Cloudflare accounts enables organizations to collaborate across one Cloudflare account, and use roles-based permissions to control access. Each user is assigned an individual API key and can use two-factor authentication to protect their own account: https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/setup/manage-members/roles/

features.name: Multi-Org Setup
features.notesEn: https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/setup/accounts-and-zones/#accounts

features.name: SSO support
features.notesEn: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/applications/configure-apps/dash-sso-apps/

features.name: Cloudflare API Access
features.notesEn: https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/api-terraform/access-api-examples/#access-api-examples

features.name: Terraform Integration
features.notesEn: https://blog.cloudflare.com/getting-started-with-terraform-and-cloudflare-part-1/

features.name: Site, DNS Analytics
features.notesEn: https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/additional-options/analytics/#analytics-and-logs

features.name: Site Analytics (resolution)
features.notesEn: The Cloudflare analytics dashboard provides all customers with 30 days of data and the ability to magnify your data by time range scopes of 1 hour, 15 minutes, or 1 minute, depending on your plan type. - Free customers can magnify down to a 1 hour scope, for the last 24 hours - Pro and Business customers can magnify down to a 15 minute scope, for the last 6 hours - Enterprise customers can magnify down to a 1 minute scope, for the last 30 minutes.

features.name: DNS Analytics (historical time)
features.notesEn: View the history of your DNS queries by up to a certain amount of time.

features.name: Firewall Analytics (historical time)
features.notesEn: Visualise and analyze all Firewall Events in one place to better understand your threat landscape. Identify, mitigate, and review attacks more effectively.

features.name: Load Balancing Analytics (historical time)
features.notesEn:
Overview metrics show the number of requests routed to specific pools within a load balancer, helping you:
- Evaluate the effects of adding or removing a pool.
- Decide when to create new origin pools.
- Plan for peak traffic demands and future infrastructure needs.
- Add additional filters for specific pools, times, regions, and origins.
https://developers.cloudflare.com/load-balancing/reference/load-balancing-analytics/#load-balancing-analytics

features.name: Audit Logs
features.notesEn: View and download the most recent changes made within your account via UI or API.

features.name: Request Logs
features.notesEn: Cloudflare Enterprise customers have access to detailed logs of HTTP requests for their domains. These logs can be helpful for debugging, identifying configuration adjustments, or creating analytics, especially when combined with other data sources, such as application server logs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpull/requesting-logs/#request-logs

features.name: Waiting Room Analytics
features.notesEn:
Waiting Room Analytics gives you historical insights into the traffic going through your waiting room compared to your waiting room settings. Data is stored for the past 30 days.
https://developers.cloudflare.com/waiting-room/waiting-room-analytics/#waiting-room-analytics 
Using Waiting Room Analytics, you can:
- Evaluate peak traffic flow through your waiting room and onto your site.
- Determine how long users spent in the waiting room.
- Use analytics to help calibrate your waiting room settin
